backward-sexp (C-M-b): move over the previous balanced s-expression
forward-sexp (C-M-f): move over the next balanced s-expression

prog-indent-sexp (prog-mode C-M-q, here = s): reindent point .. end-of-forward-sexp via the tree-sitter reindent (indent-region equivalent); with a prefix argument reindent the enclosing defun. Faithful to prog-mode.el (forward-sexp span, prefix -> beginning-of-defun).
backward-up-list (C-M-u): move backward out of the enclosing list to its open (zemacs_core::list_motion)
up-list: move forward out of the enclosing list to just past its close (zemacs_core::list_motion)

emacs forward-sentence (M-e) -> move_sentence_forward (movement::move_next_sentence)
what-line: report the line number of point
emacs indent-relative -> indent_relative: space out to under the next indent point in the previous nonblank line, else advance to the next tab stop
emacs what-page -> what_page: report the page number (^L-delimited) and the line within that page (zemacs_core::page::page_and_line)
count-lines-page (C-x l): lines on the current page, before + after point
describe-char (C-u C-x =): char glyph + dec/oct/hex code, U+ codepoint, Unicode general-category and block, buffer position; pure builder in zemacs-core::cursor_info, unit tested

transpose-paragraphs: swap the current paragraph with the previous one

vim :normal[!] {cmds} → ex_normal: parses the arg into a key sequence and replays it through compositor.handle_event (the macro-replay path), so every motion/operator/command applies.

vim :k {x} → ex_mark (alias of :mark)
vim :resize [+/-]{N} → ex_resize: relative grow/shrink or absolute height via tree.resize_vertical (height-only; :vertical resize width is best-effort).
vim :buffer {name} / :b → ex_buffer: switch to the open buffer whose path contains {name} via editor.switch; errors on no match / >1 match (E93/E94). Buffer numbers not modeled.

Jump to the [count]th quickfix error on a line above the cursor line in the current buffer; multi-error lines count as one stop, landing on the last entry of the target line (Vim :cabove). Engine: zemacs_core::quickfix::above; wired via qf_move_dir.

Jump to the [count]th quickfix error on a line below the cursor line; multi-error lines count as one stop, landing on the first entry of the target line (Vim :cbelow). Engine: zemacs_core::quickfix::below.
Jump to the [count]th quickfix error strictly before the cursor position, comparing line then column; every entry is its own stop (Vim :cbefore). Engine: zemacs_core::quickfix::before.
Jump to the [count]th quickfix error strictly after the cursor position, comparing line then column (Vim :cafter). Engine: zemacs_core::quickfix::after.

outline-mode: org-style TAB cycling of the heading at point folded -> children -> subtree, chosen from how much of the subtree is hidden (zemacs_core::outline::outline_cycle_next); folding via the document fold state

outline-mode: org global TAB cycling of the whole buffer show-all -> overview (top headings) -> contents (all headings), chosen from the current fold state (zemacs_core::outline::outline_cycle_buffer_next); reuses sublevel_folds/all_bodies

emacs global-display-fill-column-indicator-mode -> toggle_fill_column: ruler at fill/text width via config.rulers. Partial: zemacs ruler is a global config toggle, not a per-buffer minor mode.
emacs global-eldoc-mode -> signature_help: zemacs shows LSP signature help on demand only; it has no automatic idle echo-area documentation, so this is on-demand-equivalent (partial).
emacs windmove-left -> jump_view_left: focus_direction(Left) moves focus to the geometric left window in the split tree.
emacs windmove-up -> jump_view_up: focus_direction(Up) moves focus to the window above.
emacs windmove-down -> jump_view_down: focus_direction(Down) moves focus to the window below.
emacs windmove-swap-states-left -> swap_view_left: swap_split_in_direction(Left) swaps the current and left windows.
emacs windmove-swap-states-right -> swap_view_right: swap_split_in_direction(Right).
emacs windmove-swap-states-up -> swap_view_up: swap_split_in_direction(Up).
emacs windmove-swap-states-down -> swap_view_down: swap_split_in_direction(Down).
Kills the comment on the current line (count kills that many lines) to the kill ring = emacs comment-kill; tree-sitter comment node with string-aware line-comment-token fallback.

abbrev-prefix-mark (M-'): mark point as the start of an abbrev so the next expand-abbrev treats only the text after the mark as the abbrev, keeping any non-abbrev prefix. Faithful to lisp/abbrev.el: without a prefix arg it first expands any abbrev before point ((or arg (expand-abbrev))), then records the location (abbrev-start-location / ABBREV_START keyed on DocumentId) and inserts a hyphen ((insert "-")). expand_abbrev, refactored into abbrev_expand_impl, consults the location via take_abbrev_start and abbrev--before-point's logic: strip the hyphen, take text up to point minus trailing whitespace (abbrev_prefix_split) as the abbrev name, expand it, and remove a dangling hyphen when nothing matches. Bound at SPC x e m.

unexpand-abbrev: undo the last expand-abbrev, replacing the inserted expansion with the original abbrev text and moving point past it, then clearing the saved text so a repeat is a no-op. Faithful to lisp/abbrev.el (last-abbrev / last-abbrev-text / last-abbrev-location recorded by expand_abbrev; unexpand_span helper for the replace span + point-max guard). Bound at SPC x e u.
inverse-add-global-abbrev: word before point becomes the abbrev name, prompt for expansion, define in the global table (reuses emacs_abbrev::define + shared abbrev_word_before helper)
kmacro-end-or-call-macro (C-x e / F4): ends the macro if recording, else replays the last macro; bound to F4.
append-next-kill (C-M-w): the next kill joins the last kill-ring entry instead of starting a new one (appended for a forward kill, prepended for a backward kill via record_prepend); bound at SPC r a.
comint-kill-input (C-c C-u): clears the pending input line via Comint::kill_input
comint-bol-or-process-mark (C-a): move to process mark / bol, via unit-tested bol_or_process_mark_target
comint-delchar-or-maybe-eof (C-d): delete char, or close child stdin (EOF) on empty input
comint-magic-space: expand !!/!$/!*/!-N/!str history designators (unit-tested expand_history) then insert a space
comint-insert-previous-argument (M-.): insert last arg (!$) of previous command via unit-tested last_argument
comint-get-next-from-history: yanks the next-forward input-ring entry (approximates the post-yank M-n step)
comint-next-prompt (C-c C-n): scrolls to the next prompt line; comint scrollback has no separate point so this is a viewport move
comint-previous-prompt (C-c C-p): scrolls to the previous prompt line (viewport move; no buffer point)
comint-show-output (C-c C-r): scrolls the start of the last command's output to the top, via unit-tested last_prompt_line
comint-show-maximum-output (C-c C-e): scrolls newest output to the bottom
comint-delete-output (C-c C-o): deletes the last command's output lines via unit-tested last_output_range
comint-write-output: writes the last command's output (last_output_range) to a prompted file
comint-copy-old-input (C-c RET): copies the most recent submitted input onto the input line (comint has no buffer point to pick an arbitrary old input)
comint-truncate-buffer: trims scrollback to comint-buffer-maximum-size (1024) keeping the newest lines
comint-strip-ctrl-m: strips ^M (\r) from scrollback lines via unit-tested strip_ctrl_m
comint-interrupt-subjob (C-c C-c): sends real SIGINT to the child via libc::kill(child.id()) on unix; single direct child (no PTY process group)
comint-stop-subjob (C-c C-z): sends real SIGTSTP to the child via libc::kill on unix
comint-continue-subjob: sends real SIGCONT to the child via libc::kill on unix
comint-quit-subjob: sends real SIGQUIT to the child via libc::kill on unix
comint-kill-subjob: sends real SIGKILL to the child via libc::kill on unix
comint-dynamic-list-input-ring: lists the input-ring history inline in the buffer (Emacs pops a *Input History* buffer)
comint-history-isearch-backward-regexp: one-shot substring search of the input ring (previous_matching) rather than incremental regexp isearch
comint-run: prompts for a program+args and opens it in a new comint buffer (Comint::with_program)
shell-forward-command (M-f): move point forward over a ;|& -separated shell command on the input line, via unit-tested forward_command
shell-backward-command (M-b): move point backward to the start of the shell command, via unit-tested backward_command
gud-jump: sends 'jump FILE:LINE' to the inferior debugger running in the comint buffer
gud-refresh (C-l): requests a redraw and scrolls the debugger output to newest (approximates recenter/redisplay)
Pure diffmode::diff_hunk_kill deletes the hunk at point; if it is the file's only hunk the file header is removed too (matches diff-remove-hunk). Char-for-char unit-tested.
Pure diffmode::diff_file_kill deletes the whole file section (banner + ---/+++ headers + hunks) at point. Unit-tested.
Pure diffmode::diff_split_hunk splits a unified hunk at point; recomputes correct start+length for BOTH halves (stock Emacs leaves the 2nd header at a ,1 placeholder). Unit-tested.
Pure diffmode::diff_reverse_direction swaps ---/+++ paths, each @@ range and every +/- body sign; reverse-then-reverse round-trips byte-for-byte (tested).

emacs diff-context->unified: pure diffmode::diff_context_to_unified converts a context diff to unified, interleaving both sides (reads ! change lines). Round-trip tested against diff-unified->context.
emacs diff-unified->context: pure diffmode::diff_unified_to_context converts unified to context (***/--- blocks, empty side omitted). Output matches diff -c; char-for-char tested.
Strips trailing whitespace from added (+) lines within the diff buffer. PARTIAL: stock Emacs also rewrites the patched source files on disk; zemacs only edits the diff buffer.
Narrows the buffer to the hunk at point via diffmode::hunk_line_bounds + doc.narrow_to (widen to undo). The prefix-arg file variant is not wired.

Applies the hunk at point to its target file on disk: locates the old image at old_start (fallback whole-file scan), replaces with the new image, writes the file. Pure apply_hunk unit-tested (exact position, scan, and mismatch-error cases).
Applies every hunk of every file in the diff buffer to its target file (hunks applied last-to-first so line numbers stay valid). Pure apply_file_diff unit-tested.

Prompts for a name, stores name->keys in a process-global registry (zemacs_core::kmacro), and makes the name resolve through MappableCommand::from_str so it can be bound to a key and replayed. Not shown in the command palette because zemacs macro replay must be triggered from the main key loop (deferred callbacks are dropped in the palette).

Inserts a textual (fset 'last-kbd-macro (kmacro "KEYS")) definition of the last macro at point via zemacs_core::kmacro::format_kbd_macro_definition. KEYS is zemacs's key-string (round-trips through parse_macro), not Emacs format-kbd-macro spelling; inserts the last macro rather than prompting for a name.

Runs the last kbd macro once at the start of each line in the primary selection, feeding keys through the compositor. Recomputes each line start from line number rather than using Emacs markers, so a macro that changes the line count can drift; in-place per-line edits (the common case) work.
Same end-or-call behaviour as kmacro-end-or-call-macro; the sticky 'press the last key again to repeat' loop is not implemented, so it is functionally the non-repeat command.

Opens the last macro's key-string in a minibuffer prompt; on accept it re-parses and stores the edited keys as the last macro. Uses a one-line minibuffer editor, not Emacs's dedicated *Edit Macro* buffer with its annotated per-key layout.
Edits the last macro's keys in a minibuffer prompt and re-parses on accept (shared with edit-kbd-macro). Not the full *Edit Macro* buffer.
Opens the whole macro for editing like kmacro-edit-macro; per-key step-through prompting (insert/skip/replace each action) is not implemented.

Builds a macro key-string from the recent-key ring (last_keys, dropping the invoking key) and opens it in the macro editor. The recent-key ring is bounded and the reconstruction is not guaranteed to be a clean replayable macro.

Prompts for a key and reports the exact config line ([keys.normal] "key" = "@KEYS") to bind the last macro. zemacs cannot mutate the live keymap at runtime (the keymap is read-only ArcSwap behind DynAccess), so it does not install the binding itself.
No-op: zemacs redraws every frame, so there is no separate mid-macro redisplay to force. Reports itself rather than doing work.
parses the ASCII table at point into zemacs_core::table (recognize) and reports its dimensions; buffer tables are plain text so this is a re-parse, not a persistent activation
recognizes the table grid inside the current selection and reports its dimensions
recognizes the whole table at point (same parser as table-recognize)
reports the contents and WxH of the cell the caret sits in
zemacs buffer tables are plain text with no persistent recognition state (unlike Emacs text properties), so there is nothing to deactivate; command reports that
no persistent recognition state to clear (buffer tables are plain text)
no persistent recognition state to clear (buffer tables are plain text)
no persistent recognition state to clear (buffer tables are plain text)
reports current cell size, whole-table size, grid dimension and total cell count for the table at point

cycles the current column's justification left->center->right->full (Justify + justify_line, unit tested); dense grid stores justification only at render time so only the most-recently-justified column keeps its alignment
raises the current column's minimum width by 1 (Table::widen_cell, unit tested); operates on the whole column since a dense grid shares one width per column
lowers the current column's minimum width by 1, never below the content width (Table::narrow_cell, unit tested)
raises the current row's minimum height by 1 line (Table::heighten_cell, unit tested); operates on the whole row (dense grid)
lowers the current row's minimum height by 1 line, never below 1 (Table::shorten_cell, unit tested)
merges the current cell's text with its right neighbour (span_cell_right, unit tested); a dense grid cannot draw a true multi-column spanned cell, only the text is merged
splits the current cell vertically at its middle (split_cell_vertically, unit tested); the new row is inserted across every column because the grid is dense
splits the current cell into two columns at its middle (split_cell_horizontally, unit tested); the new column is inserted across every row (dense grid)
splits the current cell into two rows at its middle (split_cell_vertically, unit tested); the new row is inserted across every column (dense grid)
fills cells from point to the end of the table with an incrementing sequence (Table::insert_sequence, unit tested)
emits HTML source for the table at point below it; the pure Table::generate_source also produces LaTeX and CALS (all unit tested)
captures the selected plain text into a table (rows on newlines, cells on whitespace) via zemacs_core::table::capture (unit tested) and replaces the selection with the grid
converts the table at point back to plain text (Table::release, unit tested)
toggles and reports fixed-width mode; zemacs has no live in-buffer cell editor that reflows on typing, so the toggle has no reflow behaviour to gate
emacs picture-motion -> zemacs picture_motion: move point in drawing dir, padding
emacs picture-motion-reverse -> zemacs picture_motion_reverse: move opposite drawing dir
emacs picture-set-tab-stops -> zemacs picture_set_tab_stops: stops at word starts (picture::set_tab_stops)
emacs picture-tab -> zemacs picture_tab: move to next tab stop, padding
emacs picture-tab-search -> zemacs picture_tab_search: moves under next word-start in nearest non-blank line above; 'interesting char' heuristic is word-start only
emacs picture-open-line -> zemacs picture_open_line: split line at point, keep point
emacs picture-clear-line -> zemacs picture_clear_line: clear to end of line (no-arg form)
emacs picture-clear-column -> zemacs picture_clear_column: overwrite N cells after point with spaces (picture::clear_columns)
emacs picture-backward-clear-column -> zemacs picture_backward_clear_column: move back N cols and blank them
emacs picture-clear-rectangle-to-register -> zemacs picture_clear_rectangle_to_register: saves rectangle to register (emacs_register::set_rect) then blanks it
emacs picture-yank-rectangle -> zemacs picture_yank_rectangle: overlay killed rectangle overwriting cells (picture::overlay_rectangle)
emacs picture-yank-rectangle-from-register -> zemacs picture_yank_rectangle_from_register: overlay a register's rectangle overwriting cells
emacs 2C-split -> zemacs twocol_split: cut buffer at point col into left (in place) + right (new vsplit buffer); text transform in two_column::split_columns
emacs 2C-merge -> zemacs twocol_merge: pad left lines to separator col + append partner lines (two_column::merge_columns), then dissociate
emacs 2C-dissociate -> zemacs twocol_dissociate: break the left<->right association
emacs 2C-two-columns -> zemacs twocol_two_columns: creates a side-by-side partner buffer + association; no live scroll/point sync
emacs 2C-associate-buffer -> zemacs twocol_associate_buffer: records association with the other window's buffer; no live sync
emacs 2C-newline -> zemacs twocol_newline: inserts a newline in this column and the partner at its own point; no live point tracking
VC log for the current file (git log via the gix commit picker).
VC log for the whole repository (gix commit picker).
git log for a prompted branch, shown in a scratch buffer.
git diff HEAD for the whole working tree, shown in a scratch buffer.
git log -L<from>,<to>:<file> for the selected line range.
git log ..@{u}: commits incoming from upstream (last fetch).
git log @{u}..: commits outgoing to upstream.
git log --grep=<pattern> for a prompted pattern.
git switch -c <name>: create and switch to a new branch.
reuses the branch picker (git for-each-ref + git checkout).
git tag <name> at HEAD.
git checkout <tag/branch>, then reload open buffers.
git mv the current file, then open the new path.
git rm -f the current file.
append the file's repo-relative path to .gitignore (zemacs_core::vc::gitignore_append, unit-tested).
git checkout -- <file> to restore to HEAD, then reload the buffer.
recompute the buffer's diff base (gutter) and current-branch head via diff_providers.
alias of vc-refresh-state.
reuses git pull --ff-only (async).
reuses git push (async).
opens the Magit status buffer, zemacs's VC-directory equivalent.
opens the Magit status buffer for the project repo.
grep the project into the jumpable Run console (reuses the :grep machinery).
regex search/replace across the project (reuses :project-replace core via ripgrep).
run a synchronous shell command in the project root; output to a scratch buffer.
run an async shell command in the project root; output streamed to the Run console.
hsplit then LSP goto-definition of the symbol at point.
stages the current file then opens Magit to compose the commit; zemacs has no single check-in-comment buffer.
prompts for the project directory and opens its find-file overlay; zemacs keeps no persisted known-projects list.
lists all open buffers (reuses the buffer picker); not filtered to the current project.
opens an M-x shell-style comint buffer on $SHELL (not Emacs eshell); runs in the editor working directory.
whole-project ripgrep regex replace, not limited to the current xref result set.
whole-project ripgrep replace of the symbol (word-bounded), not the semantic LSP reference set.

indent-code-rigidly (= r): shift each region line by [count] columns, skipping lines that begin inside a string (tree-sitter string node), faithful to indent.el (in-string guard, blank lines emptied).
diary-list-entries: list the current date's diary entries; the parser now evaluates %%(diary-...) sexp entries too (zemacs_core::diary)
diary-fancy-display: reports the day's entries (dated header + text) in the echo area; zemacs has no separate *Fancy Diary Entries* buffer
diary-print-entries: reports the day's entry count; zemacs has no lpr print subsystem
diary-day-of-year: 'Day N of TOTAL; M days remaining' for today (zemacs_core::diary::day_of_year_string, unit-tested)
diary-anniversary predicate implemented + unit-tested (zemacs_core::diary::anniversary); evaluated from %%(diary-anniversary ...) entries and authored via diary-insert-anniversary-entry
diary-block predicate implemented + unit-tested (zemacs_core::diary::block); evaluated from %%(diary-block ...) entries and authored via diary-insert-block-entry
diary-cyclic predicate implemented + unit-tested (zemacs_core::diary::cyclic); evaluated from %%(diary-cyclic ...) entries and authored via diary-insert-cyclic-entry
diary-float (Nth weekday of month, +/-) implemented + unit-tested (zemacs_core::diary::float_match); genuinely evaluated by diary-list-entries from %%(diary-float ...) entries
diary-date wildcard match (t/* components) implemented + unit-tested (zemacs_core::diary::date_wildcard); evaluated by diary-list-entries from %%(diary-date ...) entries
diary-hebrew-date: today's Hebrew calendar date (reuses zemacs_core::calendar hebrew conversion)
diary-islamic-date: today's Islamic (arithmetic/civil) date
diary-french-date: today's French Revolutionary date
diary-bahai-date: today's Baha'i calendar date
diary-coptic-date: today's Coptic calendar date
diary-ethiopic-date: today's Ethiopic calendar date
diary-astro-day-number: today's astronomical (Julian) day number
diary-hebrew-omer: the Sefirat HaOmer count for today (zemacs_core::diary::hebrew_omer, unit-tested against the Hebrew calendar conversion)
diary-hebrew-rosh-hodesh: detects Rosh Hodesh (1st/30th of a Hebrew month) and names it; Emacs additionally reports Shabbat Mevarchim / the molad, which need the parashah tables

diary-hebrew-birthday: recurrence predicate implemented + unit-tested (zemacs_core::diary::hebrew_birthday); the command reports today's Hebrew date (the birth-date arg comes from the diary line). The 30-Heshvan/Adar-in-leap-year edge cases are not handled
diary-insert-monthly-entry: appends '* DAY ' (every month on today's day) to ~/diary and opens it
diary-insert-yearly-entry: appends 'Monthname Day ' (every year) to ~/diary
diary-insert-anniversary-entry: appends '%%(diary-anniversary M D Y) ' for today
diary-insert-block-entry: appends '%%(diary-block ...) '; standalone there is no calendar mark ring, so the range is today..today (edit the second date)
diary-insert-cyclic-entry: prompts for N and appends '%%(diary-cyclic N M D Y) ' for today
diary-hebrew-insert-entry: appends 'H<Hebrew date> ' for today
diary-hebrew-insert-monthly-entry: appends 'H* DAY ' (Hebrew day-of-month, every Hebrew month)
diary-hebrew-insert-yearly-entry: appends 'H<Hebrew month> <day> ' (every Hebrew year)
diary-hebrew-insert-anniversary-entry: appends '%%(diary-hebrew-anniversary M D Y) '
diary-islamic-insert-entry: appends 'I<Islamic date> ' for today
diary-islamic-insert-monthly-entry: appends 'I* DAY '
diary-islamic-insert-yearly-entry: appends 'I<Islamic month> <day> '
diary-islamic-insert-anniversary-entry: appends '%%(diary-islamic-anniversary M D Y) '
diary-bahai-insert-entry: appends 'B<Baha'i date> ' for today
diary-bahai-insert-monthly-entry: appends 'B* DAY '
diary-bahai-insert-yearly-entry: appends 'B<Baha'i month> <day> '
diary-bahai-insert-anniversary-entry: appends '%%(diary-bahai-anniversary M D Y) '
appt-add: prompts 'HH:MM message' and inserts into the sorted in-memory appointment list (zemacs_core::diary::appt_add, unit-tested)
appt-delete: removes appointments whose message matches the typed text (zemacs_core::diary::appt_delete)
appt-activate: toggles the appt-checking flag and reports the next appointment; zemacs has no idle timer, so no timed pop-up reminder is delivered

c-hungry-delete-forward (SPC x d f): skip whitespace after point (spaces/tabs/newlines) and delete the whole run, or one character if none follows, faithful to cc-cmds.el (c-skip-ws-forward then delete-region, else c-delete-function 1).

c-hungry-delete-backwards (SPC x d b): skip whitespace before point (spaces/tabs/newlines) and delete the whole run, or one character if none precedes, faithful to cc-cmds.el (c-skip-ws-backward then delete-region, else c-backspace-function 1).
emacs sgml-tag -> wrap region/point in <tag>..</tag> (prompts for tag)
emacs sgml-close-tag -> insert </tag> for innermost open element
emacs sgml-delete-tag -> delete enclosing tag pair, keep content
emacs sgml-skip-tag-forward -> move past a balanced tag group
emacs sgml-skip-tag-backward -> move back over a balanced tag group
emacs sgml-name-char -> insert &entity; for a character (numeric fallback)
emacs sgml-tag-help -> describe an HTML element (curated static table)
emacs sgml-attributes -> insert attributes at point (no DTD-driven completion)
emacs sgml-tags-invisible -> flag toggle only (no visual tag hiding)
emacs sgml-name-8bit-mode -> flag toggle only (no live re-render)
emacs sgml-validate -> shells out to onsgmls/nsgmls (external, not bundled)
emacs sgml-mode -> enter SGML mode (uses HTML grammar)
emacs html-mode -> enter HTML mode (HTML grammar + tag commands)
emacs htmlfontify-buffer -> export buffer as <pre> HTML (pure builder supports face spans; wrapper emits escaped text only)
emacs nroff-forward-text-line -> forward one text line, skip .'/request lines
emacs nroff-backward-text-line -> backward one text line, skip request lines
emacs nroff-count-text-lines -> count non-request lines in region/buffer
emacs nroff-mode -> enter nroff mode (text-line motions; no tree-sitter grammar)
emacs nroff-electric-mode -> flag toggle only (no live electric closing)
emacs tex-mode -> enter TeX mode (uses LaTeX grammar; plain-TeX not distinguished)
emacs latex-mode -> enter LaTeX mode (LaTeX grammar + tex commands)
emacs latex-electric-env-pair-mode -> flag toggle only (no live \end auto-insert)
emacs tex-region -> compiles the whole file (no temp-file region extraction)
emacs tex-file -> spawns pdflatex synchronously to a scratch buffer (no async *tex-shell*)
emacs tex-buffer -> spawns pdflatex on the buffer's file (no async *tex-shell*)
emacs tex-compile -> spawns pdflatex on the current file (no async *tex-shell*)
emacs tex-bibtex-file -> spawns bibtex on the file base name
emacs tex-view -> opens the compiled PDF in the OS viewer
emacs tex-print -> prints the compiled PDF via lpr
emacs tex-kill-job -> no persistent async job to kill (compiles run synchronously)
emacs tex-recenter-output-buffer -> output shown in scratch at compile time
Move to the next fixed-form statement, skipping continuation/comment lines; pure logic in zemacs_core::fortran, unit tested.
Move to the previous fixed-form statement; pure column-aware motion, unit tested.
Backward-match DO/IF-THEN/SUBROUTINE/... to the enclosing block opener; depth counter over statement lines, unit tested.
Forward-match the block END for the enclosing fixed-form block; unit tested.
Free-form next statement honouring '&' continuation, skipping blank/comment lines; unit tested.
Free-form previous statement, walking back over '&' continuation chains; unit tested.
Move to the next free-form block opening; unit tested.
Move to the previous free-form block opening; unit tested.
Backward-match the enclosing free-form block opener; unit tested.
Forward-match the enclosing free-form block end; unit tested.
Break the line at point onto a continuation line with the marker in column 6; pure split_line, unit tested, applied as a transaction.
Join with the following continuation line, dropping the label/marker columns; pure join_continuation, unit tested.
Toggle a leading 'C' comment on the selected fixed-form lines; pure comment/uncomment helpers, unit tested.

Re-indents the buffer by fixed-form block nesting (code at col 7, 3 cols/level); faithful to the block-nesting core of fortran-indent-line but not its every corner case (continuation indent, exotic labels).
Delete the sequence-number field (columns 73+) on every line; pure strip_sequence_nos, unit tested.
Show the fixed-form column ruler (label 1-5 / cont 6 / code 7-72 / seq 73+) in the echo area.
zemacs terminal windows cannot clip to a fixed column, so this reports the column-72 boundary instead of narrowing the window to columns 1-72.
As fortran-window-create; column clipping is unavailable in the terminal window model, so it only reports the column-72 boundary.
Enter fixed-form Fortran mode: set the document language to fortran and report status.
Enter free-form F90 mode: sets the document language to the shared fortran grammar (no separate f90 grammar) and reports status.
Live incremental whole-word search (\b-anchored word tokens); each keystroke re-searches from the start point.
Live incremental whole-symbol search; approximated with regex \b word boundaries (engine lacks Emacs \_< \_>), so symbol tokens include underscores.
Seeds the search with the word/symbol at point and jumps to its next occurrence.
Toggles regexp vs literal matching of the current isearch string and re-searches.
Toggles whole-word matching of the current isearch string and re-searches.
Toggles whole-symbol matching; over word constituents this coincides with word search (engine lacks \_<).
Toggles case-folding (smart-case when on) of the current isearch and re-searches.
Toggles lax-whitespace (a space matches a whitespace run) and re-searches.
Flag toggles but zemacs has no character-folding table, so it does not change matching.
Flag toggles but zemacs has no invisible/folded text search, so it does not change matching.
No input methods in zemacs; the command exists but only reports that it is unsupported.
Extends the search string with the next buffer character and re-searches.
Extends the search with the next word (or a char if not on a word) and re-searches.
Extends the search with the next symbol/char; reuses word-or-char (word constituents include _).
Extends the search with the next word and re-searches.
Extends the search with the next symbol; over word constituents this is the next word.
Extends the search to the end of the current line and re-searches.
Prompts for a char and extends the search with buffer text up to it.
Extends the search string with the kill-ring top (emacs kill ring) and re-searches.
Appends the kill-ring top like isearch-yank-kill; cycling the ring in place (true yank-pop) is not tracked.
Extends the search with the clipboard selection (the + register) and re-searches.
Shortens the search string by one char and re-searches.
Alias of isearch-del-char: shortens the search string by one char.
Opens a prompt prefilled with the current search string to edit, then re-searches.
Cycles to an older entry of the / search-history ring and re-searches.
Cycles to a newer entry of the / search-history ring and re-searches.
zemacs's search applies to the selection immediately; exit just ends the session (point already at the match).
Returns point to where the last isearch session started; only meaningful after an isearch-* start command.
Alias of isearch-abort: returns point to the recorded session origin.
Prompts for a character and adds it literally to the search string, then re-searches.
Completes the search string from the most-recent / history entry only (no completion list).
Adds a character by zemacs digraph mnemonic (e.g. a: -> ä); no full Unicode-name database.
Adds a character by digraph mnemonic; zemacs has no emoji-name table.
Runs occur with the current search pattern, reusing the existing occur overlay.
Hands the search pattern to a buffer-wide replace-all (do_substitute); not an interactive per-match y/n loop.
Hands the search regexp to a buffer-wide replace-all; not an interactive per-match loop.
No persistent hi-lock; sets the / search so matches show via search highlight and jumps to the first.
No persistent line hi-lock; lists matching lines via occur as the closest analog.

default-indent-new-line (M-j): break line at point and continue the comment, aligning the continuation under the current comment (comment-indent-new-line in-comment path); newline-and-indent when not in a comment.

recenter-other-window (C-M-S-l): recenter point in the other window, equivalent to C-l (recenter) acting on the other window. Ports lisp/window.el, which runs recenter-top-bottom in other-window-for-scrolling; centers the other view via align_view, restoring focus.
Move to the start of the function at point via the tree-sitter `function` text object (like mark-defun's Around object).
Move to the end of the function at point via the tree-sitter `function` text object.
Select the whole function around point via the tree-sitter `function` text object.

Move to the first token of the C statement at point; pure `;`/`{`/`}`-delimited scan in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested. Does not parse string/comment contents (documented limitation matching the common case).
Move just past the next `;`/`{`/`}` ending the statement at point; pure scan in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested (same string/comment limitation).
Move forward across the following #if/#endif preprocessor conditional; depth-counting port of c-scan-conditionals core in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested (nested + enclosing cases).
Move backward across the preceding preprocessor conditional; mirror depth-counter in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested.
Move up out of the containing preprocessor conditional (skips balanced siblings, honours a numeric prefix for levels); pure up_conditional in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested.
Re-indent the current line or every selected line to the tree-sitter indentation (reuses zemacs_core::indent::indent_for_newline per line).
Re-indent every line of the function at point (tree-sitter `function` object bounds + per-line tree-sitter reindent).
c-ts-mode variant of c-indent-defun: re-indent the whole function at point via the tree-sitter indent query.
Re-indent the balanced { } expression at/after point (brace-match to find the range, then per-line tree-sitter reindent).

Fill the // or * comment block around point to text-width, reusing the block's comment prefix; pure fill_c_comment in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested. Does not re-synthesise /* */ delimiters (fills interior body lines).

Align trailing backslash line-continuations in the region to a common column (c-backslash-column 48 / max 72), or remove them with a numeric prefix; pure align/remove/column helpers in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested.

Break the line at point: continue a // or * comment prefix, or append a `\` continuation inside a multi-line macro, else a plain indented newline; pure prefix/in_cpp_macro helpers in zemacs_core::cmode, unit tested.
Toggles and reports the auto-newline flag, but zemacs has no live cc-mode electric insertion, so no automatic newlines are inserted (flag only).
Toggles and reports the hungry-delete flag; the c-hungry-delete-* commands already work explicitly regardless of this flag (no live DEL/Backspace remap).
Toggles and reports the electric flag, but zemacs has no live cc-mode electric machinery (flag only).
Reports the tree-sitter node kind at point and the enclosing node stack; not cc-mode's own syntactic-analysis symbols (statement-cont, arglist-intro, ...), which need CC Mode's parser.
Expands the region by shelling out to `cpp -P` and shows the result in a scratch buffer; requires a cpp on PATH, there is no in-process preprocessor.
Reports the current tree-sitter indentation style/width; zemacs has no cc-mode style engine (gnu/k&r/bsd/... named styles) to select.
Toggle a breakpoint on the current line = gdb-toggle-breakpoint; reuses the existing DAP breakpoint model + setBreakpoints (dap-toggle-breakpoint).
Jump to a breakpoint's source line via the breakpoints picker = gdb-goto-breakpoint.
Make a thread current (DAP threads + select-thread) = gdb-select-thread.
Select a stack frame, jumping to it and making its scopes current (DAP stackTrace/scopes) = gdb-frames-select.
Local variables (and arguments) of the current frame via DAP scopes/variables, shown in a popup = gdb-display-locals-buffer.
Call stack of the current thread (cached DAP stackTrace frames), selected frame marked = gdb-display-stack-for-thread.
Locals of the current thread's innermost frame via DAP scopes/variables = gdb-display-locals-for-thread.
Delete the breakpoint on the current line and re-send DAP setBreakpoints = gdb-delete-breakpoint.
Set a variable/lvalue value via DAP setExpression, or an assignment evaluate when unsupported = gdb-edit-value.
CPU registers = the adapter's Registers scope; DAP has no dedicated register request, so this only works when the adapter exposes a Registers scope (many do not).
Registers of the current thread's innermost frame; same Registers-scope limitation as gdb-display-registers-buffer.
Issues the real DAP disassemble request around the selected frame PC and shows it; gated on the adapter's supportsDisassembleRequest capability (many adapters lack it).
Disassemble around the current thread's innermost-frame PC via the DAP disassemble request; gated on supportsDisassembleRequest.
Prompts for an address/expression, issues the real DAP readMemory request and hexdumps the bytes; gated on the adapter's supportsReadMemoryRequest capability (many adapters lack it).

zemacs' DAP path has no dedicated inferior-IO buffer (adapter output events surface inline), so this focuses the IDE Run console where program output lands. A true separate inferior-IO terminal is gud-gdb.
Opens the IDE debug tool window (call stack / variables / breakpoints panes); a single multi-section panel rather than Emacs' separate multi-window GDB layout.
Restores the debugger layout by reopening the IDE debug tool window (see gdb-many-windows caveat).
Sends gdb's own `complete WORD` to the inferior in the gud comint buffer; candidates print there. No in-place command-line completion like Emacs.
customize-option -> :customize-option [name]: open Settings pre-filtered to the named option (alias of :customize-variable).
customize-variable -> :customize-variable [name]: open Settings pre-filtered to the named option, where its value can be edited and persisted.
customize-browse -> :customize-browse: open the Settings tab to browse all editor options (alias of :customize).
customize-face -> :customize-face: open the Color Scheme tab (theme/face editor) where face fg/bg/modifiers are edited live and saved.
customize-create-theme -> :customize-create-theme: open the Color Scheme tab, which builds and saves a custom theme from per-scope colors (alias of :customize-face).

set-selective-display (C-x $) -> hide lines indented past the numeric prefix column (only lines with < N columns of indentation stay visible), implemented over the fold model; no prefix disables it. Pure geometry in zemacs_core::selective_display (unit-tested).

set-face-foreground -> :set-face-foreground <face> <color>: override a theme face's foreground live (hex or named color), applied via Theme::set_style + set_theme; affects UI chrome and syntax scopes.
set-face-background -> :set-face-background <face> <color>: override a theme face's background live, applied via Theme::set_style + set_theme.
global-whitespace-toggle-options -> toggle rendering of whitespace characters (whitespace.render All<->None) live in every buffer.
global-tab-line-mode -> toggle the buffer tab line (zemacs bufferline Always<->Never) live.
customize-group -> :customize-group [name]: opens Settings pre-filtered to the name; zemacs has no Customize group tree, so this is a name/substring filter over the flat option list.
customize-apropos -> :customize-apropos [text]: opens Settings pre-filtered; the filter is a case-insensitive substring match, not a full Emacs regexp.

customize-unsaved -> :customize-unsaved: opens Settings showing only options changed from the compiled default; zemacs does not track a separate set-vs-saved state, so this approximates 'unsaved' as 'differs from default'.

customize-changed -> :customize-changed: opens Settings filtered to options changed from default; zemacs has no per-version change tracking, so it shows all modified settings (alias of :customize-unsaved).
customize-saved -> :customize-saved: opens Settings filtered to modified options; zemacs cannot distinguish saved from merely-set, so both appear (alias of :customize-unsaved).

set-right-margin -> :set-right-margin [n]: zemacs has no persistent right-margin text property, so it re-fills the region to n columns narrower than text-width (the observable effect of a right margin on refill), reusing fill_paragraph.
set-fringe-style -> :set-fringe-style [none|default]: the TUI has no pixel fringe; maps to the left gutter, clearing (none) or restoring (default) the gutter layout via config.
global-visual-wrap-prefix-mode -> toggle soft-wrap with indentation carry-over (max-indent-retain); zemacs has no prefix-only mode, so continuation lines keep indentation only when soft-wrap is on.
ps-despool: print the accumulated PostScript spool via lpr
ps-print-buffer: buffer -> PostScript -> lpr

toggle_superword toggles super-word w/b/e motions: symbol-syntax characters (_, -, etc.) count as part of a word so a whole snake_case_symbol moves as one word, the inverse of subword-mode. Ports lisp/progmodes/subword.el superword-mode (which installs forward-symbol motion); reuses the long-word / WORD motion whose boundary rule joins alnum+punctuation and splits on whitespace. Mutually exclusive with subword-mode.
